items.text: Ask about onset, progression, severity, recent acute illness, and change from usual health.
Ask about oral intake, thirst, vomiting, diarrhea, urine volume, urinary retention, and recent weight change.
Ask about new or missed medications, insulin, diuretics, ACE inhibitors, NSAIDs, steroids, laxatives, and supplements.
Ask about chronic kidney disease, diabetes, adrenal or thyroid disease, prior electrolyte disorders, and baseline glucose control.
Ask about infection symptoms, ischemic symptoms, recent procedures or contrast exposure, alcohol use, and dietary change.
Ask about weakness, cramps, paresthesias, palpitations, confusion, seizures, and exercise intolerance.
Ask about polyuria, polydipsia, polyphagia, blurry vision, fatigue, weight change, recurrent infection, and dehydration.
Ask about insulin or medication adherence, pump or monitor problems, recent dose changes, access barriers, diet change, and steroid use.
Ask about nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, deep breathing, confusion, severe weakness, and markedly reduced intake or urine output.
Ask about infection, ischemic symptoms, stress, trauma, surgery, pancreatitis symptoms, alcohol use, and other substance use.
Assess appearance, heart rate, blood pressure, respiratory rate and pattern, temperature, and bedside perfusion.
Assess mucous membranes, jugular venous pressure when feasible, edema, skin turgor, and orthostatic symptoms when safe.
Assess alertness, orientation, attention, behavior, and change from baseline.
Assess rate, rhythm, heart sounds, peripheral pulses, capillary refill, and signs of volume overload or depletion.
Assess work of breathing, lung sounds, and abnormal respiratory pattern including deep or rapid breathing.
Assess gross strength, tremor, reflexes, sensation, tetany, asterixis, and gait only when safe and relevant.
Assess mucous membranes, heart rate, blood pressure, peripheral perfusion, mental status, and orthostatic symptoms when safe.
Assess respiratory pattern, fruity breath odor when present, lung findings, abdominal tenderness, and distention.
Inspect lungs, skin, feet, urinary symptoms by examination when relevant, and indwelling devices for possible infection.
Assess alertness, orientation, focal deficits, strength, and gait only when safe.
